free_text: Isobaric yield curves at A=72 from the spallation of
molybdenum isotopes by high-energy alpha particles
The Space Radiation Effects
Laboratory (SREL) synchrocyclotron.
The targets used in this study were made from (>96%)
enriched powder of 92Mo, 96Mo, and 100Mo isotopes.
Ge(Li) detector, 7.6x7.6-cm
NaI(Tl) detector, thin Ge X-ray detector, beta-
prop.=end-window gas-flow proportional counter.
The stacked foil activation method
was employed. The number of alpha particles
(including multiple traversals) striking the target
was monitored by the 27Al(alpha, X)24Na reaction.
Irradiations were typically 10 min in duration for
absolute cross section measurements and 1-3 h for
relative yield determinations (e.g., 72Zn, 66Ge).
Activated targets were usually brought back to
Carnegie-Mellon University for chemical separation
and radiation assay.

Table II, page 1987.
All data points are corrected so as to coincide with
the "A=72 curve" by multiplying each yield by the
factor exp[-px (A-72)]. In this work, the mass-yield
coefficient px, was calculated from the estimated
total isobaric yield at A=67 and A=72.
An error equal to 20% of an interpolated
yield was combined with the error of the measured
yield to give the uncertainty of the total isobaric
yield.
Table III, page 1988.
